evidence: The paper explicitly employs "permutation-based random forest feature importance by repeatedly permuting each predictor in the held-out test set and quantifying the resulting mean decrease in AUC." Figures 3-4 show "normalized permutation importances," not impurity-based importance. Because the importance method is permutation-based rather than impurity-based (Gini/MDI), the documented bias mechanism does not apply, regardless of how they interpret magnitude and ranking.
